why_them: Website last updated August 2018 — over 7 years outdated, lacks responsive mobile design, no enquiry form, and relies on obfuscated email links. Despite covering weddings, portraits, commercial and PR work across Scotland the site cannot competitively convert enquiries from mobile users. A modern rebuild with a contact form and fresh gallery would directly increase bookings.
recommended_service: Full website redesign — modern portfolio site with enquiry form, separate wedding/portrait service pages, and mobile-first layout
source: Google search: wedding photographer Dumfries Galloway

why_them: WordPress site last updated November 2022 and has no visible enquiry/contact form — a significant conversion gap for a wedding photographer. The site relies on gallery plugins that may be slow to load and the design is generic. Competitors have far more polished branding, putting Pete Miller at risk of losing enquiries to better-presented rivals.

business_name: Elite Photography & Videography by Allison Bogie
why_them: Operating on onlinepictureproof.com — a third-party client proofing platform — rather than a proper branded website. This is a generic free-tier portfolio tool, not a business website, meaning she has no SEO, no Google presence under her own domain, and no enquiry funnel. She covers Dumfries, Annan, Castle Douglas, Moffat, Stranraer and Gretna Green, indicating a serious full-time operation badly in need of a real web presence.

why_them: Running 14 years as a family/children portrait specialist with a decent-but-dated WordPress site that lacks clear pricing and a visible enquiry form. why_them: Award-winning portrait studio on Divi/WordPress that lacks a contact form and has a dated visual design that undersells its internationally recognised credentials. why_them: Operates on the Weebly drag-and-drop builder — a free/low-cost platform that is visually dated, has limited SEO capability, and signals small-scale despite offering premium sustainably grown wedding flowers. Serves the entire D&G region plus Gretna Green and into Ayrshire, so the audience reach is strong but the website fails to match the quality of the product.

why_them: Has a modern Squarespace site but uses a Gmail address for business contact — an unprofessional signal that undermines their premium sustainable flower brand. why_them: Family florist established since 1986 with a WordPress/Elementor site that has no enquiry form and no wedding gallery — two critical omissions for a shop offering bespoke bridal flowers. why_them: The only dedicated bridal boutique in the Stranraer area, serving a wide geographic catchment in west Galloway — yet the Elementor/WordPress site is sparse, showing very few dress images despite claiming over 200 gowns.
